title: linum nervosum subsp. glabratum
intro: Linum nervosum subsp. glabratum, commonly known as hairy flax, is a plant species that belongs to the flax family (Linaceae). It is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Linum nervosum subsp. glabratum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
